description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that defines three stages: phase1, phase2, and phase3, and sets the variables DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2 and DOCKER_IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/publicmarket/public-market-storefront. In the phase1 stage add a build job that uses the docker:latest image, logs in to the registry with gitlab-ci-token, builds three images (base, ci, kubectl) from their Dockerfiles, pushes each tag to $DOCKER_IMAGE, and runs docker system prune -f, with a "build" tag. In the phase2 stage add an rspec job that uses the $DOCKER_IMAGE/ci:latest image, caches vendor/bundle, node_modules and the Code Climate test‑reporter, runs a before_script to download and prepare the test‑reporter, installs Ruby gems via bundle (or bundle install --deployment), runs yarn, creates and loads the DB schema, then executes bundle exec rake parallel:spec and reports coverage with ./cc-test-reporter after-build, stores coverage/ and tmp/capybara/ as artifacts (expire 1 day, always), retries twice, runs except when the commit message matches /skip-test/, and provides postgres:10.1-alpine and elasticsearch:6.4.2 services with appropriate environment variables. In the phase3 stage add a deploy to production job that runs manually only on the master branch, uses the $DOCKER_IMAGE/kubectl:latest image, logs in to the registry, builds and pushes the production image, prunes Docker, runs several kubectl patch commands on statefulsets, checks rollout status, and sends a Slack notification via curl, with an environment named production pointing to https://production.public.market.
